01-title: HireStream — the H P S E D C Overseas Placement Portal and Mobile Application. A secure, bilingual platform connecting the youth of Himachal Pradesh to safe, regulated employment abroad. Prepared for the H P State Electronics Development Corporation by H T I S Telecom.
02-problem: Every year, thousands of young people from Himachal Pradesh look for work overseas. Too many fall into the hands of unregulated agents — paying illegal fees, signing contracts they never see, and arriving in a foreign country with no protection and no official record of their placement. HireStream exists to change that. Commissioned by H P S E D C under Work Order H P S E D C, software zero eight, twenty twenty-five, and built to the agreed Functional Requirements, HireStream is a single, government-supervised window for safe and regulated overseas recruitment. It brings four participants onto one transparent platform — the job seeker, the licensed recruiting agency, the overseas employer, and H P S E D C itself as the regulating authority — so that every step, from the first application to the final placement, is recorded, verified, and accountable.
03-overview: Here is how the four roles fit together. An overseas employer posts a verified hiring requirement. A licensed Indian recruiting agency, approved by H P S E D C, picks it up and sources candidates. A job seeker builds one rich profile and applies, guided at every step. And throughout, H P S E D C oversees the entire pipeline, with a complete and permanent audit trail. The platform is bilingual — available in English and Hindi — and runs on both web and mobile. In the walkthroughs that follow, you will see each role's journey on the live platform — including the refinements made and verified during user-acceptance testing with the H P S E D C team. Let us begin with the overseas employer.
05-bridge-agency: Section two — the recruiting Agency workflow.
06-bridge-candidate: Section three — the Job Seeker workflow.
07-bridge-admin: Section four — H P S E D C oversight.
08-built: A word on how HireStream is built — because for a government platform, the engineering matters as much as the features. It follows a clean three-tier architecture. The presentation tier is a responsive web application and native Android and i O S apps. The application tier runs on Node-dot-J S, with role-based access control, the candidate-matching engine, and a security layer of hardened sessions, rate limiting, and input validation. And the data tier is PostgreSQL, with roughly thirty-six tables and an immutable audit log. Every technology is modern, proven, and open-source — no licensing lock-in. Security is built in: role-based access control for each of the four roles, encryption in transit and at rest, bcrypt-hashed passwords, one-time passwords, and a complete audit trail. And it is built to standards — G I G W accessibility, I S O twenty-seven-thousand-one-aligned security practices, and data-protection equivalent to G D P R. The external integrations H P S E D C requires — HIM Access single sign-on, Aadhaar, DigiLocker, and the government email and S M S gateways — are all built as pluggable adapters, ready to switch on from the admin console the moment production credentials are provided. No code change required.
09-status: So where does the project stand today? The platform is built across the full contracted scope. It has been tested by the H P S E D C team through formal user-acceptance testing, and the U A T final report is on record. The forty-percent design-approval milestone has been submitted, with the complete S R S and requirement traceability. And the system is running right now on a secure staging environment — everything you have just seen is the live platform, not a mock-up. The one step remaining is to host it on government infrastructure for production.
10-ask: To take HireStream into production, we have one ask of the I T department — and we have made it precise. We request two virtual machines on the State Data Centre: a staging server and a production server, sized for the five-thousand-concurrent-user target in the F R S. We have specified the exact base software to pre-install — Ubuntu, Node, PostgreSQL, and Nginx — because we propose an air-gapped model, with the servers isolated from the internet and accessed only over S S H through your jump host. H T I S delivers the application as a pre-built artifact; no build ever runs on the server. Beyond that, we need C A-issued T L S certificates for the two domains, controlled outbound reachability to the government services the integrations call, and a standard service account and backup target. The full, itemised request is in the Infrastructure Provisioning document — designed to slot straight into your existing data-centre policies, and to make it easy to say yes.
11-close: HireStream — safe, regulated, and accountable overseas employment for the youth of Himachal Pradesh. We thank the H P I T Department, and we look forward to taking it to production. Thank you.
